copy.title: White Sox shutout Twins. Knicks-Spurs Finals Game 2 tomorrow. Monaco Grand Prix this weekend.
copy.keyTakeaway: Baseball kept it simple today—dominant pitching performances across three games. Tech stocks took a hit overnight, with NVDA and MSFT leading declines. Meanwhile, gaming and streaming dominate the culture conversation as PS5 and TV shows reshape the entertainment landscape.

todaysHits.sports: Tigers, White Sox, Mets all cruise to blowout wins Wednesday.
todaysHits.golf: Brian Campbell leads Memorial Tournament after opening round.
todaysHits.f1: Monaco Grand Prix arrives this weekend—the circus comes to town.
todaysHits.worldcup: World Cup kicks off June 11—countdown begins, group stages set.
todaysHits.culture: God of War arrives PS5; Peabo Bryson remembered; NBC cancels Hunting Party.

lead.headline: Tigers demolish Rays behind dominant pitching performance
lead.whatHappened: Detroit rolled into Tampa and won 7-2, controlling the game from start to finish. The Rays' offense never found rhythm against a Tigers pitching staff that was simply sharper.
lead.whyBullet1: Detroit's pitching dominated, holding Tampa to just two runs and dictating the entire flow of the game.
lead.whyBullet2: This is the kind of complete performance—run support plus lockdown pitching—that teams need when they're fighting for position in June.
lead.whatToSay: The Tigers showed up ready to play and didn't let off the gas. That's the kind of game you don't give back in a tight division.

sportsOther: Tigers hammered Tampa's pitching into submission with seven runs.
White Sox pitched a shutout and made Minnesota look lost.
Mets offense woke up and buried Seattle early.

markets.mood: Tech stocks got hit harder than the broad market—NVDA down 3.6% and MSFT down 3.2%—while the 10-year yield climbed 0.8%, signaling a shift away from growth names into bonds.
markets.whyBullet1: When Treasury yields spike, expensive tech stocks lose their appeal because future cash flows matter less relative to immediate bond returns. Big cap tech got punished for being crowded.
markets.whyBullet2: Traders are watching for any Fed commentary or economic data Friday that either confirms or walks back this rate-higher thesis. If inflation data comes in hot, the selling pressure extends.
markets.bringUp: Bitcoin dropped 5.1% in a single day—steeper than any equity index—which suggests investors are rotating out of risk assets across the board, not just stocks.

golf.headline: Campbell, Coody, Fox tied atop Memorial after day one
golf.whyCare1: Muirfield Village is a brutally hard test that separates real ball-strikers from pretenders, and these three are executing.
golf.whyCare2: The Memorial draws the strongest field outside the majors—this week's leaderboard tells you who's actually playing well heading into summer.
golf.watchFor: Watch if Campbell can hold the lead. He's not a household name, so if he wins, that's a real story. If he fades, see who takes over.
golf.whatToSay: The Memorial doesn't get enough respect. It's basically a fifth major. Jack Nicklaus built Muirfield to punish you, and this week someone's going to prove it.

culture.topic: Peabo Bryson, R&B Legend, Dies at 75
culture.whatHappened: Peabo Bryson, the R&B singer behind Disney's 'A Whole New World' and 'Beauty and the Beast,' died at 75.
culture.whyItMatters: He defined the sound of 90s romance — if you grew up watching those movies, his voice was literally part of your childhood.
culture.whatToSay: Man, Peabo Bryson just passed. He sang 'A Whole New World.' That guy had a voice.

culture.topic: God of War Laufey Hitting PS5 This Year
culture.whatHappened: Sony announced God of War Laufey is coming to PlayStation 5, revealed during the June 2026 State of Play.
culture.whyItMatters: This is the next major franchise entry people actually care about — it's not some spin-off, it's the real thing.
culture.whatToSay: God of War's dropping on PS5. That's the whole conversation.

culture.whatHappened: Danish crime series following a cold case unit in Copenhagen — Scandinavian noir with an odd-couple detective pairing and cases that spiral in directions you don't see coming.

copy.finalSharpTake: The tech selloff is real. NVDA and MSFT both down hard, the 10-year yield climbing—that's not rotation, that's caution. Meanwhile baseball is doing what it always does: blowouts everywhere, nobody's playing close games. The Mets, Tigers, and White Sox all won by five-plus runs today. When you see that kind of dispersion in sports and markets pulling back at the same time, the market's telling you it wants safety. Everything else is noise.
